title: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs Campaign Monitor: Side-by-Side Comparison (2025)
intro: Choosing between ConvertKit and Campaign Monitor comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is built for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while Campaign Monitor targets agencies and teams managing multiple brands. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies And Teams Managing Multiple Brands

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on creators, bloggers, and course sellers
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on agencies and teams managing multiple brands

faq.q: Is ConvertKit better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit wins for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while Campaign Monitor is stronger for agencies and teams managing multiple brands. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor starts at a lower price point ($11/mo vs $29/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Campaign Monitor to ConvertKit is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
